steps.title: Identify the formation contrast
steps.content:
The notes show two different types of igneous rock with opposite formation processes: intrusive rock cools deep within Earth's crust (found at Acadia), while extrusive rock exits the crust and cools on the surface (found at Crater Lake).

To contrast how the rocks formed at these two parks, the answer must highlight this difference in formation.

steps.title: Apply the formation contrast to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B directly shows this contrast using “whereas” — Acadia's rock cooled deep within the crust while Crater Lake's cooled after exiting it.

The other three either don't connect both parks to their specific formation processes or contradict the goal by showing similarity instead of contrast.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
